claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 1 child:
  - Alex is 21 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 21, which is 20 or over
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex
expected_eligibility.reason: Child is 21, which is 20 or over
circumstances: Child is 21, which is 20 or over
Child is not in local authority care
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Hello! I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit.

To assess your eligibility, I need to gather some information about your circumstances. Let me start by asking:

**Do you have any children, and if so, how many children are you inquiring about?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hello! Yes, I do have a child. I'm inquiring about my son Alex. He's 21 and lives at home with me.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information. I need to gather a bit more detail about Alex's situation.

**Is Alex currently in approved education or training?** (For example, is he enrolled in full-time non-advanced education such as A-levels, Scottish Highers, or an approved training course?)

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, he's not in any education or training at the moment.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for clarifying that. Let me ask one more important question:

**Does Alex have any disabilities or does he receive any disability benefits such as Disability Living Allowance or Personal Independence Payment?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, he doesn't receive any disability benefits like that.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Thank you for providing that information. Let me read the official guidance to confirm the eligibility rules before giving you my assessment.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Based on the information you've provided and the official eligibility rules, I can now make an assessment.

According to the government guidance, Child Benefit is available for children **under 16**, or for those aged **16-19** if they remain in **approved education or training**. 

In Alex's case:
- He is 21 years old
- He is not in approved education or training
- He does not receive disability benefits

Unfortunately, this means Alex falls outside the age criteria for Child Benefit eligibility.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
**I'm sorry, but you are not eligible for Child Benefit for Alex.**

The reason is that Alex is 21 years old, which exceeds the age limit. Child Benefit is only available for:
- Children under 16, or
- Young people aged 16-19 who are in approved education or training

Since Alex is 21 and not in approved education or training, he falls outside the eligibility criteria.

If you have any other questions or if there are any other children in your household you'd like to inquire about, please let me know.

reasonings: Alex is 21 years old and is not in approved education or training, nor does he receive any disability benefits. According to the Child Benefit eligibility rules, you normally qualify for Child Benefit if you're responsible for a child under 16. If the child is 16 or over, you'll normally still qualify only if the child is under 20 and they stay in approved education or training. Since Alex is 21 and not in approved education or training, he does not meet the age criteria for Child Benefit eligibility.
arguments.overall_reasoning: The claimant has one son, Alex, who is 21 years old, lives at home, and is not in education, training, or receiving disability benefits. Child Benefit is only available for children under 16, or those aged 16-19 in approved education or training. Alex exceeds the maximum age limit for Child Benefit eligibility.

child_evaluations.reasoning: Alex is 21 years old and is not in approved education or training, nor does he receive any disability benefits. According to the Child Benefit eligibility rules, you normally qualify for Child Benefit if you're responsible for a child under 16. If the child is 16 or over, you'll normally still qualify only if the child is under 20 and they stay in approved education or training. Since Alex is 21 and not in approved education or training, he does not meet the age criteria for Child Benefit eligibility.

response.overall_reasoning: The claimant has one son, Alex, who is 21 years old, lives at home, and is not in education, training, or receiving disability benefits. Child Benefit is only available for children under 16, or those aged 16-19 in approved education or training. Alex exceeds the maximum age limit for Child Benefit eligibility.
